lesson.big_idea: A fraction names equal parts of a whole. We move from shared pieces to symbols only after the meaning feels clear.

sections.title: Share First
sections.body: Have you shared a chapati fairly with a friend? Equal pieces are the heartbeat of fractions.

sections.title: Concrete Share
sections.body: Share one whole into four equal parts. Each part is one-fourth — not because of the symbol, but because the pieces match.

sections.title: See It
sections.body: Shade two of four equal parts on the diagram. The picture teaches before the numerals do.

sections.title: Worked Example
sections.body: Write two-fourths, then simplify with the same model: two shaded parts of four equal parts become one-half.

sections.title: Try This
sections.body: Draw three equal parts and shade one. What fraction did you make?

sections.title: Lesson Summary
sections.body: Equal parts make fraction meaning. You can check one drawing and feel ready for the next problem.

sections.title: Apply
sections.body: Find one food item at home you can divide into equal parts and name the fraction aloud.

diagram_package.title: Equal Parts
diagram_package.caption: A whole divided into four equal parts with two shaded
diagram_package.explanation: This visual lowers cognitive load by showing equality before symbols.
diagram_package.practice_question: Point to one equal part and name its fraction.
